rationale: The cited work is used in a specific inferential step rather than only as general background. In Study 5, the authors cite Pham and Muthukrishnan (2002) when interpreting mediation results, specifically the large reduction in mean squares after adding the mediator covariate. This is a concrete methodological precedent that supports the citing paper's process claim, even though the cited paper's substantive brand-positioning theory is not central here.
relation_description: The citing paper applies an analytic precedent from the cited paper to interpret mediation evidence. It uses the cited work to justify that a very large drop in explained variance terms after including the mediator indicates the relaxation effect is mediated by construal-level thinking.
